chapter_title.ar: حَدِيثُ نُوحٍ ( عليه السلام ) يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ
chapter_title.en: HADEETH OF NOAH<sup>as</sup> ON THE DAY OF JUDGEMENT

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (6 / 318)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (6 / 318)
text: قَالَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اللَّهِ (عليه السلام) إِنَّ الْمَشْيَ لِلْمَرِيضِ نُكْسٌ إِنَّ أَبِي (عليه السلام) كَانَ إِذَا اعْتَلَّ جُعِلَ فِي ثَوْبٍ فَحُمِلَ لِحَاجَتِهِ يَعْنِي الْوُضُوءَ وَ ذَاكَ أَنَّهُ كَانَ يَقُولُ إِنَّ الْمَشْيَ لِلْمَرِيضِ نُكْسٌ.

en.hubeali: H 14892 – Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad Bin Isa, from Abu Yahya Al-Wasity, from one of our companions who said:

Abu Abdullahasws said that; ‘The walking is detrimental to the sick. When myasws fatherasws became sick, heasws had to be carried in a cloth for hisasws need, meaning the ablution, and that is why heasws used to say that the walking is detrimental to the sick’.
en.sarwar: 445. Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Ibn Abu Umeyr, from Ibn Azina that: A man came up to Abu Abdullah (a.s.) and said, ‘I saw (a dream) as if the sun emerging upon my head apart from my body’. So he (a.s.) said: ‘You will achieve a huge matter, and ‘وَ نُوراً سَاطِعا’ً a brilliant light, and a comprehensive Religion. Had it covered you, you would have been submerged in it, but it only covered your head. But, have you not read: “[6:78] Then when he saw the sun rising, he said: Is this my Lord? Is this the greatest? So when it set”, Ibrahim (as) distanced himself (as) from it’. I said, ‘May I be sacrificed for you (a.s.), they are saying that the sun is a Caliph or a king’. So he (a.s.) said: ‘I (a.s.) do not see how you can achieve the Caliphate and there was never a king in your forefathers and your ancestors. And which Caliphate and kingdom is greater than the Religion, and ‘النُّورِ’ the Light which you hope would make you to enter into the Paradise? They are mistaken’. I said, ‘May I be sacrificed for you (a.s.), you (a.s.) have spoken the truth’.
